command_snippets:
python - <<'PY'
# Example pseudo-run: run the notebook reproducibly (replace with your environment's notebook runner)
import nbformat, nbconvert
# execute notebook or run the sensitivity script embedded in notebook
PY

git add [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] attempts/baseline/actual_outputs.json && git commit -m "Add +/-15% sensitivity results and final_decision_table"
files_to_add_or_update: [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] (updated with +/-15% sensitivity findings and expert-review flag)
extra_model_fields.notes: If flips occur under +/-15%, set sensitivity_rule_expert_review_flag=true and include clear explanation and suggested reviewer actions. If no flips occur, document that explicitly and include the generated decision table.
verification_checks_before_resubmission: Open [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] and confirm schema and presence of flips if any.
Ensure research_note.md references the decision table and documents the sensitivity method and results.
Run notebook headless to confirm it writes the final_decision_table artifact.
Confirm actual_outputs.json metadata_json.validation.sensitivity_classification_flips and sensitivity_rule_expert_review_flag reflect results.

planned_changes: Modify alpha_decay_notebook.ipynb to add/adjust a reproducible sensitivity routine that: a) applies +/-15% perturbations to each normalized component independently and b) applies +/-15% stress to the priority threshold; record all per-record priority scores for each perturbation.

Execute the updated notebook and capture results; generate [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] (or .json) containing columns: record_id, baseline_priority, baseline_breach(bool), perturbed_scenario, perturbed_priority, breached(bool), flip(bool).

Update [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] to include: the +/-15% sensitivity methodology, key tables summarizing flips (if any), an explicit sensitivity_rule_expert_review_flag (true/false) and rationale, and a pointer to [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]

Update attempts/baseline/actual_outputs.json (or the new attempt's actual_outputs.json) metadata_json.validation fields: sensitivity_classification_flips (true/false), sensitivity_rule_expert_review_flag (true/false), and a short summary of flipped records if any.

If sensitivity flips affect remediation selection, re-run the budget-constrained remediation search in the notebook and update the recommended_option_set and recommended_cost_usd accordingly; document changes in research_note.md and final_decision_table.
Confirm alpha_decay_notebook.ipynb runs end-to-end in the environment and writes all declared artifact files (final_decision_table, updated research_note.md if auto-generated segments, and any logs).
Run a quick self-check: verify artifact refs include research_note.md, alpha_decay_notebook.ipynb, final_decision_table, agent_trace.json, and updated actual_outputs.json before re-submitting.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Fix disqualifying rubric violation by performing the required +/-15% sensitivity analysis, updating artifacts and metadata, and adding a machine-readable final_decision_table and explicit expert-review flags if flips occur.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Fix disqualifying rubric violation by performing the required +/-15% sensitivity analysis, updating artifacts and metadata, and adding a machine-readable final_decision_table and explicit expert-review flags if flips occur.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The submission produced the required research_note and documented priority logic and sensitivity testing at +/-5%, and deterministic artifact checks passed. However the rubric requires sensitivity analysis at +/-15% (component-level and threshold stress). The grader flagged this as a disqualifying violation (final score 0.0). The verifier confirmed the disqualifier is grounded in the provided artifacts and also flagged internal inconsistencies in the grader payload (mixed pass/fail signals). To pass, re-run sensitivity tests at +/-15%, update artifacts (notebook, research_note.md, actual_outputs.json), embed a machine-readable final_decision_table, and explicitly set expert-review flags if any classification flips occur.
